title: Triage verdicts (PRD §7, FR-5.2)
description: Point-by-point triager output: one verdict per finding. `finding_id`/`verdict`/`reasoning`/`action` are normative per PRD §7. `confidence` (severity-aware escalation, plan P4 / review F-009), `target_artifact` (upstream-invalidation routing, BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #6 / FR-10.4), and `escalated` (engine-recorded) are P4 schema additions; the PRD §7 excerpt itself is unchanged.

confidence.description: Triager self-assessed confidence; `low` escalates to a stronger model or the human gate (review F-009).

target_artifact.description: null unless the fix lands in a different artifact than the one reviewed (e.g. a plan finding implicating the PRD); set, FR-10.4 upstream-invalidation routing becomes explicit instead of inferred from reasoning text. Required-but-nullable for native structured-output strict mode (see findings.json note).

escalated.description: Engine-recorded: this verdict was re-issued by the escalation agent (or flagged for the human gate). Not produced by the cheap triager itself.
